title: Commercial office, Type V-B, standard-run stair geometry outside the IBC limits (hard fail)
notes: Hard-fail twin of office-vb-stair-geometry-violation. The plan declares Stair type: standard, which closes the spiral/winder/alternating-tread (and stair-side-handrail-guard) exceptions, so the four geometry rules assert hard violations instead of needs_review. Magnitudes differ from the soft case to add variance: tread 9 in (< 11), riser 8 in (> 7), guard 36 in (< 42), handrail 40 in (> 38 — exercises the HIGH side of the two-sided range). Stair width and corridors are clean true negatives.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Number of stories: 2
Building height: 28 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 80
Stair type: standard
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 48 in. Corridor width: 60 in (lobby).
Stairway width: 48 in. Tread depth: 9 in. Max riser: 8 in. Guard height: 36 in. Handrail height: 40 in.
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south).
WC 4 LAV 4.

ground_truth.rationale: Tread 9 in below the 11 in IBC 1011.5.2 minimum; standard stair type closes the spiral/winder exception — hard fail.
Riser 8 in over the 7 in IBC 1011.5.2 maximum; standard stair type closes the exception — hard fail.
Guard 36 in below the 42 in IBC 1015.3 minimum; standard stair type closes the stair-side-handrail-guard exception — hard fail.
Handrail 40 in above the 34-38 in IBC 1014.2 range (high side); standard stair type closes the exception — hard fail.
48 in clears the 44 in IBC 1011.2 minimum
48 / 60 in clear the 44 in IBC 1020.3 minimum
6,000 sf per story within the B/V-B allowable
